span.p:
Does the team already ship
productively on an engine with
no hard blocker for this project?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Hard requirement that rules
an engine in or out?
(source access, console SKU,
license/royalty ceiling, fidelity bar)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
UNITY (lean)
C#, deepest mobile + asset-store ecosystem,
broad device reach [verify-at-use - licensing terms]

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
UNREAL (lean)
C++ + Blueprints, top-end rendering,
source access, strong for AAA / large teams

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
GODOT (lean)
GDScript/C#, MIT (no royalty), light footprint,
excellent 2D; 3D maturing [verify-at-use]
